text: Clean code is writing that is easy to read and understand. Good naming conventions are important; choose clear, descriptive names for variables and functions. Code should act like a story, not a puzzle. Break your program into small, focused functions, each doing one thing. This approach improves readability and simplifies testing. When you return to fix a bug or add a feature, well-named functions make your code easy to modify. Clear, structured code saves time and reduces errors, making your projects more maintainable for you and your team.
